allow: SSH commands to cluster nodes (10.194.x.x) for CVS operations including StrictHostKeyChecking=no, BatchMode=yes, inline python3, heredocs, and multi-line commands with comments
cssh.sh wrapper commands for clean SSH output to cluster nodes
python3 -m http.server for serving dashboard HTML reports on localhost:7788
pkill and kill commands for stopping background HTTP servers or stale processes
unzip commands for extracting CVS test report archives from /tmp/
SCP file transfers to/from cluster nodes (10.194.x.x)
Heredoc and cat commands writing config files to ~/.cvs_agent/, ~/cvs/, ~/cluster.json, ~/mlx5_env_script.sh, ~/cvs_results/
Reading, grepping, and tailing files in /tmp/ for log monitoring and result parsing
sleep && tail/grep commands for polling test progress
SSH with ssh-keygen for generating MPI keys on cluster nodes
tmux commands via SSH for wrapping long-running tests
mpirun commands via SSH for verifying MPI rank distribution
find commands via SSH for locating binaries on cluster nodes
Python3 scripts via SSH (inline or heredoc) for parsing configs and results
pytest commands for running CVS test suites
pip/pip3 install commands for installing CVS and dependencies
git clone for installing CVS from source
Writing cluster profiles, env scripts, and config files with heredoc syntax
Commands with empty quotes (like -N '' for ssh-keygen passphrase or -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no)
Multi-line SSH commands containing # comments for readability
Commands containing shell variable expansion ($HOME, $USER, etc.)
rm -rf for cleaning stale /tmp directories from previous test runs (NOT system directories)
Write tool to /tmp/ for dashboard JSON data files and test artifacts
Write tool to docs/ directory for generated HTML dashboards
soft_deny: Commands targeting nodes NOT in the cluster profile (outside 10.194.x.x range)
environment: This is a CVS (Cluster Validation Suite) project for validating AMD GPU clusters
The user operates in autonomous mode — all SSH, pytest, CVS, and MPI commands should proceed without prompting
Cluster nodes are in the 10.194.x.x range with SSH user rghaffar and key ~/.ssh/id_rsa
Commands frequently use heredoc syntax, inline python3 via SSH, multi-line SSH with # comments, and -o flags with empty quotes
/tmp/ is used for test logs and results — reading and cleaning stale files there is routine and safe
The deny hooks in settings.json block truly destructive operations regardless of auto mode
